description: Scope the autopilot §1 dirty-tree guard and all restore paths to autopilot's owned harness surface, so stray foreign edits neither block a run nor get destroyed by the restore. Approach B (scope to owned paths); the restore becomes a two-step that switches HEAD then force-resets only owned paths. clean-restore.sh is rewritten in lockstep and a new owned-surface-guard.sh probe is added.

userStories.title: Define the owned-surface constant in §1
userStories.description: As the autopilot skill, I need a single canonical list of the paths I mutate so the §1 guard and the restore can both reference it.
acceptanceCriteria: In .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md §1 (Guardrails, the 'Require clean state' block), define a single OWNED_PATHS shell variable exactly once, before the clean-state check uses it
OWNED_PATHS enumerates exactly these space-separated paths: .claude/ context/ docs/ scripts/ crons/ wiki/ evals/ memory/ tasks/ CHANGELOG.md — no more, no fewer
grep -c 'OWNED_PATHS=' .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md returns exactly 1

A '# NOTE:' comment immediately near the definition warns that OWNED_PATHS MUST be used UNQUOTED (bare $OWNED_PATHS) so word-splitting yields multiple pathspecs; quoting it ('"$OWNED_PATHS"') would collapse to one pathspec matching nothing and make the check vacuously pass

A short note records that every tracked path autopilot writes in §2-§7 (tasks/, evals/, memory/, CHANGELOG.md, .claude/) is within OWNED_PATHS, committed by Ralph on the feature branch, or under /tmp — so the owned surface is known-complete
All shell/markdown code fences in §1 remain well-formed (the skill still renders)

userStories.title: Scope the §1 MAIN clean-state check to owned paths
userStories.description: As the autopilot skill, I want my §1 dirty-tree check to inspect only owned paths so a stray edit elsewhere does not block me.
acceptanceCriteria: The MAIN §1 clean assertion that emits 'ERROR: dirty working tree' is replaced with path-scoped forms: 'git diff --quiet -- $OWNED_PATHS && git diff --cached --quiet -- $OWNED_PATHS' (OWNED_PATHS unquoted)
The §1 self-heal stranded-branch block (the 'if [ "$BRANCH" != "development" ] && git diff --quiet ...' conditional) is left tree-wide and NOT scoped

A '# NOTE:' in the skill documents that the self-heal and MAIN check are now different predicates: the self-heal force-checks-out only when the WHOLE tree is clean, so it can never fire while foreign WIP is present and therefore never destroys foreign WIP
No tree-wide 'git diff --quiet' without a '-- ' path argument remains in the MAIN owned-surface check (the self-heal conditional line is the only permitted tree-wide git diff --quiet)
Manual repro documented/verifiable: with an unrelated .codex/config.toml edit staged, the MAIN §1 check evaluates clean and does not take the exit-1 branch

userStories.title: Emit BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP on a genuine owned-surface block
userStories.description: As an operator watching the heartbeat, I want a dirty-owned-state block to be visibly distinct from a generic failure so a stall is not mistaken for idleness.
acceptanceCriteria: When §1 finds the OWNED surface dirty, the run does not proceed and emits the liveness token BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP to crons/.cron.log and the memory-log Result: field — not bare FAIL
The string 'BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP' appears in the §1 failure path of .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md
The .cron.log liveness append is guarded so a missing/unwritable log does not crash the skill (e.g. trailing '2>/dev/null || true'), matching the existing liveness convention
A stray edit OUTSIDE the owned surface does not reach this block (covered by US-002 — it proceeds normally)

userStories.title: Non-destructive two-step restore scoped to owned paths
userStories.description: As the autopilot skill, I want my restore to clean only my own owned-path residue, return HEAD to development, and leave foreign WIP untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: Every restore site that used tree-wide 'git checkout -f development' (the §7 finalize code block, the §5 RALPH-INCOMPLETE prose, the §6 PR-DRAFT-EVAL-RED prose, and the Guidelines 'canonical clean restore' paragraph) is replaced with the canonical scoped restore two-step: first 'git checkout development -- $OWNED_PATHS' (discard own owned-path residue), then 'git checkout development' (switch HEAD)
The scope-restore step MUST precede the branch-switch step (it clears residue that would otherwise make a non-forced 'git checkout development' refuse)

The post-restore assertion verifies BOTH owned-paths-clean ('git diff --quiet -- $OWNED_PATHS && git diff --cached --quiet -- $OWNED_PATHS || { echo "ERROR: autopilot restore left a dirty owned tree"; exit 1; }') AND HEAD on development ('[ "$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD)" = "development" ] || { echo ...; exit 1; }')
The 'git restore --source=' content-only alternative is NOT used anywhere
After the change, grep -c 'git checkout -f development' .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md returns exactly 1 (only the §1 self-heal keeps the forced tree-wide form)
No 'git add -A' or 'git add .' remains that could stage a foreign file; any staging is path-scoped

A documented note states the scoped restore touches only TRACKED files, so an untracked owned-path orphan from a mid-run crash is not auto-removed (git clean deliberately NOT added — too destructive); such orphans are cleaned manually

A documented note states a foreign change (e.g. .codex/config.toml), modified or staged, survives the restore byte-for-byte (left in place / left staged) and is ignored by the scoped assertion and next §1 check

userStories.title: Document BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP in token table + §8 log enum
userStories.description: As a maintainer reading the skill, I want the new token documented alongside the others.
acceptanceCriteria: A BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP row is added to the '### Status tokens' table in SKILL.md with an accurate one-line description (dirty owned surface; run skipped until the owned surface is clean; non-destructive to foreign WIP)
BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP is added to the §8 memory-log 'Result:' enumeration list
grep -c 'BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP' .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md returns at least 3 (failure path + token table + §8 enum)

userStories.title: Rewrite evals/probes/clean-restore.sh for the scoped restore
userStories.description: As the eval suite, I must assert the new scoped-restore invariant so the board does not green-to-red, while still encoding the 'own residue force-discarded at every exit path' lesson.
acceptanceCriteria: evals/probes/clean-restore.sh no longer asserts '>= 3' occurrences of 'git checkout -f development' nor 'no bare git checkout development'

It asserts the canonical scoped restore is present: a minimum count (>= 3) of the literal 'git checkout development -- $OWNED_PATHS', AND the scoped post-restore assertion message 'restore left a dirty owned tree', AND the HEAD assertion substring 'rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD'
It asserts exactly 1 'git checkout -f development' remains and that it is the self-heal (distinguished by adjacent BRANCH/rev-parse context within a small line window)
The probe keeps its 'tier: A' marker and updates '# desc:' and '# source:' to cite issue #63
The probe is a 3-state oracle: exit 0 PASS, exit 1 REGRESSION, exit 2 SKIPPED (skill absent)
Probe exits 0 against the updated SKILL.md and exits 1 against an unmodified SKILL.md (verifiable: stash the skill edit or test against git show origin/development:.claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md)

userStories.title: Add evals/probes/owned-surface-guard.sh
userStories.description: As the eval suite, I want a probe that proves the §1 guard and restore are scoped to owned paths.
acceptanceCriteria: New executable file evals/probes/owned-surface-guard.sh (chmod +x), with 'tier: A' and a '# desc:'/'# source:' citing issue #63

Static assertions against .claude/skills/autopilot/SKILL.md using exact literal grep patterns (mirroring clean-restore.sh): (a) 'git diff --quiet -- $OWNED_PATHS' present AND the MAIN check has no unscoped tree-wide git diff --quiet (self-heal 'if [ "$BRANCH" != "development" ]' line exempt); (b) 'BLOCKED-OWNED-WIP' present; (c) 'git checkout development -- $OWNED_PATHS' present AND 'rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD' present
3-state oracle: exit 0 PASS, exit 1 REGRESSION, exit 2 SKIPPED (skill absent)
Probe exits 0 against the updated SKILL.md AND exits 1 against an unmodified SKILL.md (symmetric oracle, matching US-006)

userStories.title: Refresh evals/RESULTS.md via the probe suite
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the benchmark to reflect the new and rewritten probes, all green.
acceptanceCriteria: Run the probe suite (evals/run.sh or /eval) and regenerate evals/RESULTS.md — do not hand-fake it
evals/RESULTS.md lists 'owned-surface-guard' as green and the rewritten 'clean-restore' as green
No previously-green probe is newly red as a result of this change
